summary:
Administered one unit of PRBC usually results in an increase of approximately 3% in HCT according to multiple reliable medical resources; thus supporting selection of Option C. The core finding remains unchanged post-research review emphasizing its validity within standard hematology practice guidelines regarding RBC transfusions impacting patient’s circulating volume fractions measured via HCT tests. To summarize Agent2's reasoning into two sentences focused on key clinical points:

One unit of PRBC generally causes an elevation of around 3% in HCT across numerous studies and medical texts. This information confirms the appropriateness of selecting Option C without altering our conclusion after further investigation.
